source.description: Six-panel generated pencil process sheet cropped into step frames and finished art.
source.notes: Honest backdated archive lesson; selected as a distinct geometric object lesson, not a timely claim.

process_strategy: The circular case, face, needle, hinge loop, tick marks, and shadow all appear before the finished image so the final panel only darkens and shades established parts.

final_elements.name: round compass case
final_elements.description: A round pocket compass case drawn from two loose concentric circles.

final_elements.name: inner face circle
final_elements.description: A smaller circle inside the case that holds the compass face.

final_elements.name: needle diamond
final_elements.description: A simple two-point diamond needle crossing the center.

final_elements.name: top hinge loop
final_elements.description: A small loop and hinge cap at the top of the compass case.

final_elements.name: face tick marks
final_elements.description: Short tick marks around the compass face, without readable lettering.

final_elements.name: case shadow and graphite tone
final_elements.description: Soft graphite shading on the case edge and cast shadow.

frames.visible_job: Draws loose circles for the outer compass case.
Adds the inner face circle and center point.
Adds a simple diamond needle across the center.
Adds the small hinge and hanging loop at the top of the case.
Adds face tick marks plus soft case and cast-shadow tone.

final_step.visible_job: Only darkens keeper contours, clarifies the needle and ticks, and strengthens graphite shadows on established shapes.

rejection_checks: No hinge, needle, tick marks, or shadow should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
